zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of optimizing a black-box function defined over the output space of a deep generative model. This is a challenging task because the function is typically expensive to evaluate and the generative model is complex. We propose a new method, Weighted Retraining (WR), which addresses this problem by iteratively retraining the generative model on a weighted dataset of samples that have high function values. We show that WR is sample-efficient and can be used to optimize functions in high-dimensional spaces. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on a variety of tasks, including optimizing the properties of molecules and generating images with specific attributes.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 33 (NeurIPS 2020)

extra.Editors: H. Larochelle and M. Ranzato and R. Hadsell and M. F. Balcan and H. Lin

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 33 (NeurIPS 2020)
